uiGuidelines.description: All components in Neuctra UI are fully customizable using className-based styling. Developers are encouraged to use className, contentClassName, and related style props to refine spacing, typography, colors, shadows, and layout behavior. This flexibility allows building modern, visually polished, and production-grade interfaces without modifying core components.
uiGuidelines.designPrinciple: Use className overrides intentionally to enhance visual hierarchy, spacing balance, and modern UI aesthetics. Prefer subtle shadows, proper spacing (padding/gap), and consistent color tokens for a clean and attractive interface.
colorRules: ONLY use colors from the provided theme tokens.
Never use hardcoded Tailwind colors such as text-blue-500, bg-red-500, border-gray-200, etc.
Never use inline hex colors (#000000, #ffffff, etc.).

Always use semantic token classes such as bg-primary, text-primary, bg-background, bg-accent, bg-muted, border-border, text-foreground, text-muted-foreground, text-destructive, and text-primary-foreground.
Primary buttons should typically use bg-primary text-primary-foreground.
Cards and secondary surfaces should prefer bg-muted or bg-accent.
Error or warning states should use text-destructive.
The generated UI must remain theme-compatible and automatically adapt to light and dark modes through CSS variables.
strictRules: Do not invent new theme tokens.
Do not use arbitrary color values.
Do not use Tailwind color palettes.
All color styling must come from Neuctra theme tokens.

components.name: Button
components.description: Interactive button component with support for states, loading indicators, icons, and custom styling overrides.

size.description: Size variant mapping to padding and font sizes.

loading.description: Shows a loading spinner and disables user interaction.

loadingText.description: Text displayed alongside the loading spinner.

fullWidth.description: Stretches the button to fill its container width.

iconBefore.description: Icon element rendered before button children.

iconAfter.description: Icon element rendered after button children.

disabled.description: Disables interaction and applies disabled styles.

className.description: CSS class for the outer button element.

contentClassName.description: CSS class for internal flex content wrapper.

iconClassName.description: CSS class for internal icon containers.

loaderClassName.description: CSS class for the loader spinner.

textClassName.description: CSS class for children wrapper text.

examples.label: Primary Button
examples.code: <Button variant="default">Get Started</Button>

examples.label: Outline Button with Icon
examples.code: <Button variant="outline" iconBefore={<Plus size={16} />}>Add Item</Button>

examples.label: Loading Button
examples.code: <Button loading loadingText="Saving...">Save Changes</Button>

bestPractices: Use the primary ('default') variant for main call-to-actions.
Limit primary buttons to one per view/section.
Use 'outline' or 'ghost' for secondary actions.
Always provide an iconBefore or iconAfter to increase button visual cues when appropriate.
agentHints: Compose inside ModalFooter or DrawerFooter for CTA actions.
Pair ghost buttons with Badge counts for interactive notifications.
Set fullWidth=true when displaying actions in mobile drawers or full-width forms.

components.name: Text
components.description: Typography wrapper component that maps semantic tags and handles casing transform, color mapping, truncate, and line decorations.

as.description: The HTML element or tag to render the text as.

italic.description: Applies italic styling to the text.

truncate.description: Limits text to a single line, ending with an ellipsis.

color.description: Predefined theme colors or custom color value.

examples.label: Heading 1 Text
examples.code: <Text as="h1" color="primary" className="text-2xl font-bold">Welcome Home</Text>

examples.label: Truncated Description
examples.code: <Text as="p" color="muted" truncate>This is a long description text that will be truncated on small viewports.</Text>

bestPractices: Always use appropriate HTML tags (h1-h6 for titles, p for paragraphs, span for inline) for accessibility (SEO).
Do not style colors manually when using standard colors (default, primary, muted).
agentHints: Use 'as="a"' to automatically get default link styles, external link tags, and underline formatting.
Use 'color="muted"' for secondary descriptions or metadata lines.

components.name: Image
components.description: An advanced Image component with responsive sizing, ratios, aspect ratios, overlays, error fallbacks, and click interactions.

width.description: Image container width. Can be a number, CSS string, or responsive object.

radius.description: Border radius of the image container.

overlay.description: Custom content rendered over the image.

clickable.description: Enables hover state overlays indicating clickable behavior.

examples.label: Standard Image
examples.code: <Image src="https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1506744038136-46273834b3fb?w=600" aspectRatio={16/9} radius="12px" shadow />

examples.label: Image with Hover Overlay
examples.code: Zoom In} />

bestPractices: Provide alternative text 'alt' to all images for accessibility.
Use aspectRatio to avoid content layout shifts (CLS) during loading.
Prefer loading='lazy' for below-the-fold images.
agentHints: Use inside cards alongside Title/Description for visual-heavy layouts.
Set objectFit='cover' for profile banners and square grid thumbs.

components.name: Container
components.description: Layout wrapper component that centers content and provides optional padding. It is intentionally minimal and behaves like a div. Instead of relying on predefined size tokens (sm, md, lg), use className for full layout control including max-width, spacing, and responsiveness.

padding.description: Optional internal padding utility for quick spacing control.

center.description: Centers the container horizontally using auto margins.

className.description: Primary styling hook. Use this to control max-width, responsive layout, spacing, grid/flex behavior, and overall container design.

examples.label: Page Container
examples.code:
<Container className="max-w-6xl mx-auto px-4">
  <Text as="h1">Inside Container</Text>
</Container>

bestPractices: Do NOT rely on size tokens like lg, md, xl for layout control.
Use className to define max-width, responsive breakpoints, and layout behavior (e.g. max-w-5xl, mx-auto, px-4).
Treat Container as a lightweight div wrapper rather than a rigid layout system.
Use padding prop only for quick spacing, not for full layout design.
Prefer Tailwind-style utility classes in className for modern and precise UI control.
agentHints: Use Container as a base layout shell and control everything via className.
Combine with flex/grid utilities inside children instead of modifying Container structure.

components.name: Avatar
components.description: Visual representation of a user or identity, support initials, fallbacks, statuses, and responsive sizing.

alt.description: Alt description used for screen readers and initials fallback.

isOnline.description: Adds a green online indicator dot.

isOffline.description: Adds a grey offline indicator dot.

ring.description: Adds a border ring around the avatar.

fallback.description: Explicit initials to render when src is loading or broken.

statusPosition.description: Corner placement of the status dot.

examples.label: Online Avatar
examples.code: <Avatar src="https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1534528741775-53994a69daeb?w=100" alt="John Doe" isOnline />

examples.label: Initials Fallback
examples.code: <Avatar alt="Sarah Connor" size="lg" ring />

bestPractices: Supply alt names so initials fallback works automatically.
Ensure standard contrast for ring offsets when nesting over colorful background elements.
agentHints: Use inside Navbar items or user profile headers.
Pair with Badge indicators in chat or dashboard sidebar lists.

components.name: Badge
components.description: A small badge status component supporting counts, dots, pulses, and click events.

iconPosition.description: Position of the icon relative to text.

rounded.description: Uses full circular rounded-full style vs rounded-md.

notificationDot.description: Displays small notification indicator dot in top-right.

pulse.description: Animates the notification dot with a ping pulse.

count.description: Numeric count value. Will absolute position badge over children if wrapped.

examples.label: Status Badge
examples.code: <Badge text="In Progress" variant="soft" />

examples.label: Notification Dot with Pulse
examples.code: <Badge text="Inbox" notificationDot pulse />

bestPractices: Use soft variant for low-priority categorization.
Use outline variant for secondary classifications.
Use solid or pulse badges for urgent alerts or unread counters.
agentHints: Render beside navigation labels to show unread counters.
Pair with user avatars or icon buttons to indicate pending actions.

components.name: List
components.description: An extremely rich and fully-featured List component for tree views, menus, cards, collapse drawers, shortcuts, and custom rendering.

items.description: Structured data array for items, including nested subItems/items arrays.

variant.description: Styling variants. 'menu' and 'nav' add selection effects; 'card' adds border cards.

showTree.description: Renders hierarchical connector lines for nested child items.

showDividers.description: Inserts subtle separator borders between list items.

interactive.description: Enables hover click states and transitions.

examples.label: Interactive Sidebar Menu
examples.code: <List variant="menu" density="compact" items={[{ id: 1, text: "Dashboard", icon: <Home /> }, { id: 2, text: "Settings", subItems: [{ id: 3, text: "Profile" }] }]} />

bestPractices: Use compact density for data lists, comfortable for dashboard nav cards.
For links, specify 'href' inside the item configuration so a semantic <a> tag is generated.
agentHints: Create settings lists, nav drawers, and multi-tier side menus with List.
Use showTree=true to represent folder trees or tag hierarchies clearly.

components.name: Dropdown
components.description: A flexible floating action menu component triggered by any element. Supports icons, separators, danger states, per-item customization, controlled/uncontrolled behavior, alignment control, width configuration, and full styling overrides using className and style props.

trigger.description: Element that toggles the dropdown menu when clicked.

items.description: Array of menu items or separators. Each item can include label, icon, onClick, href, danger, disabled, separator, className, and style.

align.description: Controls dropdown alignment relative to trigger element.

width.description: Width of the dropdown panel in pixels.

closeOnClick.description: Automatically closes dropdown when an item is clicked.

open.description: Controlled open state for external state management.

onOpenChange.description: Callback triggered when dropdown open state changes.

className.description: Wrapper class for trigger container styling.

menuClassName.description: Styling class for dropdown panel container.

itemClassName.description: Default styling applied to all menu items.

menuStyle.description: Inline style overrides for dropdown panel.

style.description: Inline styles for wrapper trigger container.

examples.label: User Profile Menu
examples.code:
<Dropdown
  trigger={<Avatar src="/user.jpg" />}
  items={[
    { label: "My Profile", onClick: () => {} },
    { label: "Settings", onClick: () => {} },
    { separator: true },
    { label: "Logout", danger: true, onClick: () => {} }
  ]}
/>

examples.label: Styled Dropdown
examples.code:
<Dropdown
  trigger={<button>Open Menu</button>}
  width={260}
  menuClassName="rounded-xl shadow-lg"
  itemClassName="font-medium"
  items={items}
/>

bestPractices: Use separators to visually group related actions.
Use danger flag for destructive actions like delete or remove.
Use icons in items to improve action clarity.
Prefer onClick over href since items render as buttons.
Use controlled mode (open + onOpenChange) for advanced UI flows.
Use className, menuClassName, and itemClassName for modern UI customization instead of modifying core component behavior.
agentHints: Use for contextual actions like profile menus, table row actions, and navbar utilities.
Combine with Avatar or Button components for consistent UI patterns.
Ensure trigger elements are keyboard accessible for better UX.

components.name: Alert
components.description: Context-based alert and notification system (toast) supporting soft, light, and dark variants with automated dismiss timeouts.

toast.description: Toast trigger utility. Calling toast.success('Saved'), toast.error('Error'), toast.warning(), toast.info() creates messages dynamically.

ToastOptions.type: { title?: string, description?: string, type?: 'success'|'error'|'warning'|'info', variant?: 'soft'|'light'|'dark', duration?: number }
ToastOptions.description: Config parameters passed to toast methods.

examples.label: Triggering Toasts
examples.code:
const { toast } = useToast();
// Call toast.success("Update Success", { description: "Record updated successfully." });

bestPractices: Limit toast descriptions to single short sentences.
Avoid setting duration to 0 unless the user must read and explicitly dismiss a critical error message.
agentHints: Always wrap the top-level App shell with <ToastProvider>.
Invoke toast notifications after form submissions, settings modifications, or asynchronous requests finishes.

components.name: Accordion
components.description: Collapsible drawer layout panels that show or hide sections of content, with options for multiselect, animations, and custom open items.

items.description: Structured item array with { title, content } elements.

allowMultiple.description: Enables opening more than one accordion drawer section concurrently.

defaultOpen.description: Indexes of accordion items open by default.

duration.description: Framer motion collapse transition duration in seconds.

examples.label: FAQ Accordion
examples.code: <Accordion items={[{ title: "What is Neuctra UI?", content: "It is a modern, responsive React UI package built with Tailwind variables." }]} />

bestPractices: Use Accordions for FAQs or secondary layout configurations to clean up visually crowded pages.
Make sure titles are concise, descriptive strings.
agentHints: Ensure lists are keyboard-accessible (accordions automatically support native keyboard focus toggle).
Set defaultOpen={[0]} to open the first entry when rendering instructions or initial forms.

components.name: Checkbox
components.description: Form checkbox control that supports both standalone binary state and multiple value check group options.

mode.description: Enables standalone boolean state vs multi-selection array option modes.

options.description: Available option checkboxes in group mode.

selectedValues.description: List of active checked strings in group mode.

onChange.description: Selection array change callback in group mode.

label.description: Text label shown alongside standalone checkbox.

error.description: Displays inline helper error messages below components.

examples.label: Standalone Checkbox
examples.code: <Checkbox label="I accept the terms" checked={val} onCheckedChange={setVal} />

examples.label: Checkbox Group
examples.code: <Checkbox mode="group" options={[{ label: "React", value: "react" }, { label: "Vue", value: "vue" }]} selectedValues={grp} onChange={setGrp} />

bestPractices: Use standalone single checkbox for simple binary consent actions.
Provide a descriptive 'error' state text if validation fails on form submissions.
agentHints: Compose inside vertical stacked forms.
Combine with helper text to build checkbox labels that require extended explanations.

components.name: RadioGroup
components.description: Form radio group selection control that allows picking a single value from list options, with custom icons, layouts, and sizes.

options.description: Selection options containing labels, values, descriptions, and custom item icons.

orientation.description: Lay out radio options vertically or in columns.

examples.label: Horizontal Radio Group
examples.code: <RadioGroup orientation="horizontal" options={[{ label: "Basic", value: "basic", description: "Free plan" }, { label: "Pro", value: "pro", description: "Premium options" }]} selectedValue={plan} onChange={setPlan} />

bestPractices: Use RadioGroup instead of Select if the options are few (under 5 items) to reduce form click effort.
Use horizontal orientation for compact card-style plans.
agentHints: Place descriptions inside option items to create elegant modern radio selection lists.
Bind selection to state triggers to change layouts on the fly.

components.name: Switch
components.description: An interactive switch toggle control, matching checkbox APIs for standalone binary states and selection groups.

mode.description: Standalone toggle vs switch selection group mode.

selectedValues.description: List of active values in group mode.

onChange.description: Selection array change callback in group mode.

label.description: Toggle label text in single mode.

onCheckedChange.description: Toggle change callback in single mode.

examples.label: Toggle Switch
examples.code: <Switch label="Enable Notifications" checked={notify} onCheckedChange={setNotify} />

bestPractices: Use Switch for toggles that apply immediately (like theme toggle or instant feature activations).
Prefer Checkbox over Switch inside long forms where values are not saved until the user clicks Submit.
agentHints: Pair inside settings grids or profile panels.
Set iconSize to fit constraints in small mobile layout templates.

components.name: Drawer
components.description: A flexible sliding panel component that appears from any screen edge (left, right, top, bottom). Supports structured sub-components and headless rendering via renderContent. Designed for sidebars, settings panels, mobile sheets, and complex interactive layouts.

isOpen.description: Controls whether the drawer is visible.

onClose.description: Callback fired when drawer should close (overlay click, escape key, or internal close action).

children.description: Static drawer content rendered inside DrawerContent structure.

renderContent.description: Headless render function that gives full control over drawer content and receives a close handler.

position.description: Controls the screen edge from which the drawer appears.

disableOverlayClose.description: Prevents closing drawer when clicking on the overlay backdrop.

overlayClassName.description: Custom class for styling the backdrop overlay.

overlayStyle.description: Inline style override for overlay background and effects.

examples.label: Basic Drawer
examples.code:
<Drawer isOpen={open} onClose={() => setOpen(false)}>
  <DrawerContent>
    <DrawerHeader title="Drawer Title" onClose={() => setOpen(false)} />
    <DrawerBody>Drawer content goes here</DrawerBody>
    <DrawerFooter>
      <Button onClick={() => setOpen(false)}>Close</Button>
    </DrawerFooter>
  </DrawerContent>
</Drawer>

examples.label: Headless Drawer (renderContent)
examples.code:
setOpen(false)} renderContent={(close) => (

Custom UI

)} />

bestPractices: Always provide onClose to ensure proper overlay and escape handling.
Use renderContent for advanced layouts like dashboards, filters, or dynamic forms.
Prefer left for navigation sidebars, right for settings panels, and bottom for mobile sheets.
Use disableOverlayClose for critical workflows where accidental closing must be prevented.
Use overlayClassName with blur utilities (e.g. backdrop-blur-sm) for modern UI aesthetics.
agentHints: Use DrawerTriggerButton to reduce boilerplate for open/close state management.
Combine DrawerBody with forms, filters, or analytics panels for structured UX.
Use DrawerFooter for action buttons like Save/Cancel to maintain consistency.

components.name: Select
components.description: A rich, search-enabled dropdown selector component supporting multi-select list modes, search input filters, item descriptions, and success/error status helpers.

options.description: List of options, each containing label, value, description, and custom items icon.

searchable.description: Injects search filters on long lists.

helperText.description: Displays validation support text below components.

examples.label: Searchable Select
examples.code: <Select searchable label="Country" options={[{ label: "United States", value: "us" }]} value={country} onValueChange={setCountry} />

bestPractices: Turn on searchable=true for options lists exceeding 8 items.
Configure multi-select labels appropriately when utilizing Select with 'multiple' values.
agentHints: Use on forms to capture taxonomy fields, categories, or country settings.
Combine with helperText to guide form data entry limits.

components.name: Input
components.description: A comprehensive text input component supporting email, text, search, passwords (with built-in visibility toggle), icons, prefix text, and form helper statuses.

label.description: Input title label shown above text field.

required.description: Marks field with red required indicator dot.

helperText.description: Label hint text shown below component borders.

examples.label: Password Field
examples.code: <Input type="password" label="Password" required helperText="Must be at least 8 characters." />

examples.label: Price prefix input
examples.code: <Input type="number" prefix="$" placeholder="0.00" />

bestPractices: Always specify a clear placeholder and helper text to optimize form usability.
Use prefix strings to restrict or display format units directly in inputs.
agentHints: Automatically manages show/hide buttons when type='password' is configured.
Incorporate prefixIcon or suffixIcon (e.g. Search, Lock) to create familiar intuitive interface styles.

components.name: Textarea
components.description: Rich multiline textarea editor that handles auto-resize rows, max limits, character counts, and chat submit-on-enter configurations.

autoResize.description: Automatically adapts rows height on content input.

maxRows.description: Maximum height limit before overflow scroll triggers.

submitOnEnter.description: Sends form onSubmit callback when Enter key is pressed, ignoring newlines.

onSubmit.description: Triggered when submitOnEnter is active and key is entered.

examples.label: Auto-growing Text Area
examples.code: <Textarea label="Feedback Summary" minRows={3} maxRows={8} autoResize maxLength={500} />

bestPractices: Enable autoResize to prevent annoying vertical scroll scrollbars on short forms.
Add autoResize and submitOnEnter to build text inputs that act like modern chat app textboxes.
agentHints: Pair with Button elements to construct chat window feeds.
Use helperText along with error status triggers to show validation checks on long copy sections.

components.name: Modal
components.description: A flexible and composable overlay dialog system for confirmations, forms, and complex interactions. Supports structured layout components and quick integration via ModalTriggerButton. Includes animation support, scroll locking, ESC close behavior, and full styling control via className and style props.

isOpen.description: Controls whether the modal is visible.

onClose.description: Triggered when modal should close (ESC key or overlay click).

disableOverlayClose.description: Prevents modal from closing when clicking the overlay background.

className.description: Wrapper class for modal overlay container.

style.description: Inline styles for modal overlay container.

overlayClassName.description: CSS class for overlay background styling.

overlayStyle.description: Inline styles for overlay background (blur, opacity, etc.).

examples.label: Action Dialog Modal
examples.code:
<Modal isOpen={open} onClose={() => setOpen(false)}>
  <ModalContent onClose={() => setOpen(false)}>
    <ModalHeader title="Confirm Delete" onClose={() => setOpen(false)} />
    <ModalBody>
      Are you sure you want to delete this resource?
    </ModalBody>
    <ModalFooter>
      <Button variant="ghost" onClick={() => setOpen(false)}>Cancel</Button>
      <Button onClick={onDelete}>Delete</Button>
    </ModalFooter>
  </ModalContent>
</Modal>

examples.label: Modal with Trigger Button
examples.code:
<ModalTriggerButton
  modalContent={(close) => (
    <Modal>
      <ModalContent onClose={close}>
        <ModalHeader title="Hello" onClose={close} />
        <ModalBody>Dynamic modal content</ModalBody>
        <ModalFooter>
          <Button onClick={close}>Close</Button>
        </ModalFooter>
      </ModalContent>
    </Modal>
  )}
>
  Open Modal
</ModalTriggerButton>

bestPractices: Always pass onClose to ModalContent and ModalHeader for consistent close behavior.
Use ModalTriggerButton for simple modal flows to reduce state boilerplate.
Use ModalButton for async actions like API calls with loading states.
Enable disableOverlayClose for destructive or critical workflows.
Use overlayClassName with blur utilities (e.g. backdrop-blur-sm) for modern UI aesthetics.
Avoid placing heavy applications inside modals; keep them focused and task-specific.
agentHints: Use ModalHeader + ModalFooter structure for consistent dialog UX.
Use ModalButton for async-safe submit actions (auto loading + close handling).
Combine Modal with forms, confirmations, and delete flows for optimal UX patterns.
Prefer structured mode for simple dialogs and ModalTriggerButton for rapid implementations.

components.name: Table
components.description: Flexible data table element component supporting stripes, hovers, border lines, responsive overflow tables, and row click listeners.

responsive.description: Wraps table inside scroll container to prevent layout breakages on mobile.

hoverable.description: Highlights active table rows on hover.

bordered.description: Adds dividers between rows and cells.

dense.description: Reduces padding padding to display dense datasets.

examples.label: Interactive Data Table
examples.code:
{}}>
NameStatus
Jane Cooper

bestPractices: Set responsive=true on all tables to prevent horizontal site scrolling on smaller screens.
Enable striped=true to make reading large multi-column text records easier.
agentHints: Combine columns TD data with Badge and Avatar controls to construct gorgeous user management directories.
Set row onClick listeners to open detailed panels in drawers or modals on selections.

components.name: Tabs
components.description: Rich tab navigation layout with responsive collapsing drawer/scroll list modes, custom borders, shapes, and color configurations.

position.description: Tab layout placement relative to content panels.

variant.description: Tabs border and background shape variants.

fullWidth.description: Stretches tab list selections to fill wrapper bounds.

mobileVariant.description: Mobile screen layout strategy. 'drawer' falls back to dropdown, 'scroll' scrolls, 'stack' columns items.

examples.label: Interactive Tab Panel
examples.code:
<Tabs variant="underline">
  <TabList>
    <Tab index={0}>Overview</Tab>
    <Tab index={1}>Security</Tab>
  </TabList>
  <TabPanels>
    <TabPanel index={0}>Dashboard Summary Content</TabPanel>
    <TabPanel index={1}>Password Management Content</TabPanel>
  </TabPanels>
</Tabs>

bestPractices: Use underline variant for settings pages, solid/pill variants for secondary dashboards tab configurations.
Ensure children Tab and TabPanel index parameters match exactly to keep tabs in sync.
agentHints: Utilize mobileVariant='drawer' or 'scroll' to maintain gorgeous layout proportions in mobile dashboard designs.
Configure keepMounted=true inside <TabPanel> to prevent React from unmounting states inside tabs when switching.

components.name: ThemeToggleButton
components.description: Self-contained interactive button to toggle between light and dark visual modes, featuring animated icons.

context.description: Underlying state control containing current mode and updater method.

examples.label: Theme Switcher Toggle
examples.code: <ThemeToggleButton context={{ isDark, toggleTheme }} />

bestPractices: Render inside navigation header settings elements to make theme selection easily discoverable.
agentHints: Ensure context parameters are connected to the main App color mode framework states.
